description_long: This is a mid-sized passenger jet painted primarily in white with red accents and the text 'RZECZPOSPOLITA POLSKA' along the fuselage, indicating it is a Polish government or state aircraft. The airplane features a low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and red-tipped wingtips, as well as two rear-mounted jet engines integrated into the fuselage near the tail. The tail is a T-tail design, with the horizontal stabilizer mounted atop the vertical fin. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible along the side for passengers. The aircraft's surface appears smooth and metallic, with visible panel lines and insignia on the wings and tail.
description: A white and red Polish government jet with swept low wings, rear-mounted engines, and a T-tail, featuring a forward cockpit and passenger windows.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the nose, cockpit windows, full left wing, and clear 'RZECZPOSPOLITA POLSKA' text on the fuselage.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the twin rear-mounted engines, T-tail, and the underside of the left wing.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the right side of the fuselage, engines, and the T-tail structure.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the nose, cockpit, right wing, and the prominent red and white livery.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with slight dihedral and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin rear-mounted jet engines integrated into the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear (not fully visible but implied by design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
